consistency.school_and_effect: The school is Illusion, and the effect creates a 'phantasmal image of a creature's worst fears,' which is consistent with illusion magic.
consistency.components_and_level: Level 3 spells commonly have V, S, and M components, which is standard for this level.
consistency.duration_and_mechanics: Concentration up to 1 minute is standard for powerful control spells that require sustained focus.

quality.clarity: The description is generally clear. The cone measurement (30-foot cone) is explicit.
quality.completeness: All standard spell elements (casting time, range, components, duration, description, classes) are present.
quality.flavor: The flavor text regarding projecting fears is evocative and fits the spell name.

mechanics.saving_throw: Requires a Wisdom saving throw, appropriate for resisting mental/emotional effects.
mechanics.frightened_movement: The movement clause ('must take the Dash action and move away from you by the safest available route') accurately reflects the standard frightened condition rules, adding the requirement to Dash.
mechanics.escape_clause: The condition can end early if the creature achieves line of sight break and succeeds on a subsequent Wisdom save at the end of its turn. This provides a clear mitigation path, preventing indefinite lockdown if the target can maneuver strategically.
